- [ImuOrientation.h](C:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/lib/ImuOrientation/ImuOrientation.h)
- [ImuOrientation.cpp](C:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/lib/ImuOrientation/ImuOrientation.cpp)
No more build poking from me on this one.

user_prompt: it built, but a bit too quick, can you check the latest build artifacts to see if it reflects the new ImuOrientation?
agent_reply: I checked the latest `.pio/build/thefly` artifacts. It does **not** look like `ImuOrientation.cpp` made it into the firmware.
Evidence:
- No `ImuOrientation.cpp.o` found under `.pio/build/thefly`.
- No `libImuOrientation.a` or build subdirectory for it.
- No `ImuOrientation` symbols found in `firmware.map` / build outputs.
- `idedata.json` does list `lib\ImuOrientation` as a compatible library, so PlatformIO sees it, but nothing is pulling it into the dependency graph.

So the quick build makes sense: the new library exists, but no compiled source currently references it. To force it in, something in `src/` or another used library needs to include/use it, for example by calling `ImuOrientation::init()` / `poll()` from the app loop or relevant manager.

user_prompt: implement `void test_imu()`, start the Serial port, loop and poll ImuOrientation repeatedly, every 200ms provide a printout of all available data with millisecond timestamps
agent_reply: Implemented `test_imu()` in [src/tests/test_imu.cpp](c:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/src/tests/test_imu.cpp:1).
It now:
- Starts `Serial` at 115200.
- Initializes M5 with internal IMU enabled.
- Calls `ImuOrientation::init()`.
- Polls `ImuOrientation` continuously.
- Prints every 200 ms with `millis()` timestamp, poll/sample counts, IMU enabled state, orientation id/name, upside-down flag, roll, and pitch.
Verified with:
```powershell
pio run -e thefly
Build passed, and `ImuOrientation.cpp` is now actually compiled and archived into `libImuOrientation.a`.

user_prompt: For the M5Stack Core2 as we have, what are all the I2C addresses being used?
agent_reply: Using 7-bit I2C addresses, the Core2 internal bus is SDA `21` / SCL `22` per [inc/pins.h](</d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/inc/pins.h:16>).
Expected/active Core2 addresses:
| Address | Device | Notes |

| `0x34` | AXP192 PMIC | Used by M5Unified and directly by our speaker GPIO2 fallback in [AudioManager.cpp](</d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/AudioManager/AudioManager.cpp:1018>). Core2 v1.1 AXP2101 is also `0x34`. |
| `0x38` | FT5x06 / FT6336 touch | M5GFX Core2 touch config uses this. |
| `0x51` | BM8563 / PCF8563 RTC | M5Unified RTC default. |

| `0x68` | MPU6886 IMU | Conditional: our code notes the IMU is on a daughterboard and may disappear with another codec board; it probes internal then external I2C in [ImuOrientation.cpp](</d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/ImuOrientation/ImuOrientation.cpp:1>). |
Also on our schematic, but not currently implemented in firmware:
| Address | Device | Notes |

| `0x0A` | SGTL5000XNLA3 external audio codec | Schematic connects `CTRL_DATA`/`CTRL_CLK` to the I2C control bus in [hardware/thefly.sch](</d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/hardware/thefly.sch:2571>). The address comes from the NXP SGTL5000 datasheet: the 20-QFN part uses `0001010(R/W)`, i.e. 7-bit `0x0A`. Our `AudioManager` still says external I2S codec setup is not implemented. |

So if you run an I2C scanner on this exact setup, I’d expect `0x34`, `0x38`, `0x51`, `0x68`, and possibly `0x0A` if the SGTL5000 board is installed and powered. Source for SGTL5000 address: [NXP SGTL5000 datasheet](https://cache.nxp.com/docs/en/data-sheet/SGTL5000.pdf).
